schema.captures: Top-level client industry/vertical of the fictional brand the brief serves.
schema.allowed_values: One of 22: food_beverage | hospitality_travel | fashion_apparel | beauty_cosmetics | jewelry_luxury_goods | health_wellness_fitness | medical_pharma | tech_software_saas | finance_insurance | real_estate_property | retail_ecommerce | education_edtech | arts_culture_music | sports_recreation | automotive_transport | home_interior_decor | professional_services | nonprofit_civic_govt | events_entertainment | media_publishing | manufacturing_industrial | other
schema.why: Primary corpus map + top-level buyer filter ('all beauty tasks'); closed enum keeps counts comparable and prevents vertical over-concentration when scaling to 100+.

schema.captures: Specific niche within the parent domain, from the published per-domain closed list; value must belong to the chosen domain.
schema.allowed_values: Member of the parent domain's fixed list (each list ends in an 'other_<domain>' escape). FULL TABLE: food_beverage:{coffee_roaster,craft_brewery,restaurant,bakery_patisserie,packaged_cpg,wine_spirits,food_truck,catering,other_food_beverage}; hospitality_travel:{hotel_resort,airline,tour_operator,vacation_rental,cruise,travel_agency,other_hospitality_travel}; fashion_apparel:{streetwear,activewear,formalwear,footwear,accessories,kids_apparel,denim,other_fashion_apparel}; beauty_cosmetics:{skincare,makeup,haircare,fragrance,spa_salon,other_beauty_cosmetics}; jewelry_luxury_goods:{fine_jewelry,watches,leather_goods,bespoke_atelier,other_jewelry_luxury_goods}; health_wellness_fitness:{gym_studio,supplements,yoga_wellness,nutrition_coaching,wearables,other_health_wellness_fitness}; medical_pharma:{clinic_hospital,pharma_drug,medical_device,dental,telehealth,other_medical_pharma}; tech_software_saas:{mobile_app,dev_tool,fintech_app,ai_ml,cybersecurity,enterprise_saas,gaming,consumer_hardware,other_tech_software_saas}; finance_insurance:{retail_bank,investment_wealth,insurance,crypto_web3,accounting,other_finance_insurance}; real_estate_property:{residential_listing,commercial_leasing,luxury_estate,rental_property,property_developer,architecture_firm,other_real_estate_property}; retail_ecommerce:{marketplace,dtc_brand,department_store,subscription_box,pop_up_shop,other_retail_ecommerce}; education_edtech:{k12_school,university,online_course,tutoring,language_learning,other_education_edtech}; arts_culture_music:{museum_gallery,music_label,theater_performing,festival,artist_portfolio,other_arts_culture_music}; sports_recreation:{pro_team,outdoor_adventure,esports,equipment_brand,recreation_facility,other_sports_recreation}; automotive_transport:{car_dealership,ev_brand,auto_parts,logistics_shipping,rideshare_mobility,other_automotive_transport}; home_interior_decor:{furniture,home_goods,interior_design_studio,appliances,garden_outdoor,other_home_interior_decor}; professional_services:{law_firm,consulting,marketing_agency,hr_recruiting,it_services,other_professional_services}; nonprofit_civic_govt:{charity_ngo,government_agency,advocacy,community_org,religious_org,other_nonprofit_civic_govt}; events_entertainment:{wedding_event_planner,concert_tour,film_tv,conference_expo,nightlife_venue,other_events_entertainment}; media_publishing:{news_outlet,magazine,book_publisher,podcast,content_creator,other_media_publishing}; manufacturing_industrial:{industrial_equipment,packaging_supplier,construction,chemicals_materials,b2b_components,other_manufacturing_industrial}; other:{other}
schema.why: Tight buyer slices ('screen-print tasks for craft_brewery') and niche-difficulty curriculum grouping; captures asset conventions a domain alone hides.

schema.captures: The concrete finished-artifact category the task produces; pick the enum member whose FINISHED-ARTIFACT noun matches the brief's named deliverable.
schema.allowed_values: One of 24: product_shot | hero_image | social_post | social_story | banner_ad | poster_flyer | business_card | brochure | catalog_page | menu | certificate | badge_label | packaging | mailer_postcard | logo_mark | signage | tshirt_screenprint | data_merge_set | infographic | presentation_slide | email_header | video_clip | audio_clip | other. RULE: use 'other' only when no listed member shares the artifact's production path, and record a free-text note. DISAMBIGUATION: badge_label=affixable ID/product label vs certificate=ceremonial full-page award; catalog_page=repeating product grid within a multi-item book vs brochure=folded standalone marketing piece; mailer_postcard=addressable mailed card vs social_post=screen-native square/feed; table-tent/sticker-sheet/gift-card/lookbook=other+note.
schema.why: Buyer filter by output artifact independent of industry; artifact-balance and curriculum grouping. Closed enum replaces the semi-free noun and now determines canvas_class/output_modality via a fixed map so the three cannot contradict.

schema.captures: The nature of the agent’s job — create / edit / analyze (create = assemble-from-supplied-parts, not generative).
schema.allowed_values: One of 7: create_only | edit_only | analyze_only | create_edit | create_analyze | edit_analyze | create_edit_analyze
schema.why: Coarse behavioral slice for RL-environment selection; fixes the combos into a closed enum instead of a free combo string.

schema.captures: Distinct categories of files supplied in the client hand-over, one entry per category actually present.
schema.allowed_values: Subset of: photo_raster | logo_vector | vector_art | csv_data | roster | template_indd | template_ai | template_express | font_file | video_footage | audio_track | brand_kit | reference_comp | stock_query | copy_text | none. 'none' is exclusive (only when nothing supplied); template flavor (indd/ai/express) kept distinct because it changes execution path.
schema.why: Core sliceable INPUT descriptor — 'all tasks handing over CSV+template' vs 'photo-only'; drives family/operation cross-checks and execution_mode.

schema.captures: Total number of independently-openable leaf files handed over (a FILE count, not a category count and not a data-row count).
schema.allowed_values: Integer 0..1000. COUNTING RULE: count each independently-openable file at the leaf level AFTER unzipping any archive — a 12-file brand-kit zip = 12; a multi-tab .xlsx = 1; a 20-page PDF = 1; a folder = the number of leaf files inside; 0 = nothing supplied (fully stock-or-template-sourced).
schema.why: Sizes ingestion/coordination burden independent of operation; separates single-asset quick jobs from multi-asset/folder batch packages. Distinct from input_asset_types (category count) and roster_record_count (data rows).

schema.captures: Condition of the SINGLE WORST-defect supplied raster the agent must work from, on a measurable remediation ladder (pick the level of the single worst defect present).
schema.allowed_values: 1_pristine = print-ready, no correction needed; 2_clean = single global tweak <=~10% (levels/crop only); 3_usable = one routine correction pass, defects visible only at 100% (mild noise, <=~1 stop exposure error, slight WB cast); 4_degraded = multiple corrections OR one heavy correction (>1 stop under/over-exposure, obvious color cast, visible dust/scratches, low contrast); 5_salvage = structural damage (tears, heavy grain, blown/clipped channels) requiring restoration before any styling; n_a = no supplied raster.
schema.why: The crispest difficulty driver for O1/O4; the measurable single-worst-defect rule removes the 3-vs-4 grader split and orders a restoration curriculum.

schema.captures: Resolution/size class of the HIGHEST-resolution supplied raster or footage (take the max across supplied rasters), bucketed to check against the output spec.
schema.allowed_values: web_low (<=1080px long edge) | web_hi (1081-2048px) | print_sd (2049-4000px) | print_hd (4001-8000px, A4/A3@300) | ultra (>8000px or 4K+ video) | vector_scalable (source is vector/logo, resolution-independent) | n_a (no raster/footage input).
schema.why: Catches the upscaling infeasibility trap (web_low input vs print_hd output demands a banned tool); feeds the derived resolution-gap check against resolution_tier.

schema.captures: For data-merge tasks, the number of data rows/records in the supplied roster (each producing one merged output); 0 when not a data-merge.
schema.allowed_values: Integer 0..N (0 = not a data-merge; bucketed downstream: 0 none · 1-10 tiny · 11-50 small · 51-200 medium · 201+ large).
schema.why: The volume driver unique to O5 that est_calls conflated with call count; scales merge difficulty and prices batch work. Distinct from input_asset_count and output_count.

schema.captures: The single dominant operation defining the task's core deliverable, parsed from the golden trajectory's tool sequence via the fixed tool->operation map.
schema.allowed_values: Exactly one of: O1_tonal_grade_restore | O2_masked_recolor_isolation | O3_video_audio | O4_preset_retouch_lookdev | O5_data_merge_layout | O6_stylized_duotone | O7_vector_screenprint | O8_stock_hero. TIE-BREAK (computable, in order): (1) op producing the primary output artifact (the file counted in deliverable_type/output_modality); (2) op with the most sub_operations entries mapping to it; (3) human picks and records a one-line reason.
schema.why: Anchor for corpus mapping and the operation-balance quota at 100+; deterministic tie-break makes it reproducible; human only overrides on genuine ties.

schema.captures: All other O1-O8 operations established in the golden trajectory besides the primary, by a bright-line rule.
schema.allowed_values: Subset of {O1_tonal_grade_restore,O2_masked_recolor_isolation,O3_video_audio,O4_preset_retouch_lookdev,O5_data_merge_layout,O6_stylized_duotone,O7_vector_screenprint,O8_stock_hero} EXCLUDING primary_operation; may be []. RULE: an op is secondary iff the golden trajectory contains >=1 connector call whose tool maps to that op under the fixed tool->operation map, EXCLUDING crop_resize and canvas_reframe (the [cross] sub_operations), which never establish an operation on their own.
schema.why: Lets buyers request genuinely multi-op tasks and lets curriculum grade cross-op composition; the mechanical exclusion of crop/resize removes the 'materially' ambiguity.

schema.captures: Total distinct O1-O8 operations exercised (primary + secondaries). Derived cache field; never hand-set.
schema.allowed_values: Integer 1-8. Derived = 1 + len(secondary_operations). 1 = single-op; >=3 = heavily cross-operation.
schema.why: Cheap composition/difficulty signal and a portfolio single-op-vs-multi-op balance knob; feeds sft_value_tier.

schema.captures: Set of the four families spanned by all operations in the task, capturing cross-family reach. Derived cache field.
schema.allowed_values: Subset of {Photo&Image, Vector&Print, Layout&Data, Motion&Audio}, size 1-4. Derived via fixed map: O1,O2,O4,O6,O8->Photo&Image; O7->Vector&Print; O5->Layout&Data; O3->Motion&Audio.
schema.why: Identifies rare, valuable cross-family tasks and drives family-balance quotas; size>1 makes the retired mixed_modality boolean redundant.

schema.captures: Granular atomic editing actions performed in the golden trajectory, from a CLOSED controlled vocabulary mapped to parent ops.
schema.allowed_values: Subset of: [O1] exposure_correct,contrast_adjust,highlight_recover,shadow_lift,white_balance_correct,color_temperature_shift,auto_tone,straighten,dust_scratch_removal,fade_restore; [O2] mask_subject,mask_hair,mask_by_prompt,background_remove,invert_selection,region_recolor,single_color_saturation,solid_area_fill; [O4] preset_apply,hsl_grade,vibrance_saturation,dodge_burn_light,dodge_burn_dark,skin_retouch,blemish_removal,lens_blur,gaussian_blur; [O6] duotone_map,monochrome_tint,color_overlay,halftone_screen,grain_add,noise_add,glitch_stylize; [O7] vectorize_trace,screenprint_separations,spot_color_reduce,halftone_seps; [O5] data_merge,template_fill,text_fill,variable_image_place,layout_render_export,pdf_to_indd_convert,idml_export; [O3] video_trim,video_cut_highlights,video_resize,video_frame_extract,audio_denoise,speech_enhance,media_summarize; [O8] stock_search,stock_license,stock_place; [cross] crop_resize,canvas_reframe. Must be non-empty (>=1). Closed vocabulary — additions require a schema version bump. NOTE: a required-asset derivation (vectorize a logo for screen-print, produce spot separations) shows here as its named sub_op AND sets feasibility per the derivation; transient by-products (masks, cutouts) are ordinary sub_ops and do NOT affect feasibility.
schema.why: Finest SFT/RL signal: request exact micro-skills, teach one atomic skill at a time, slice evals by micro-competency; also carries the retired input_completeness=needs_derivation signal.
schema.improves_on: operation (adds atomic sub-action layer; absorbs needs_derivation)

schema.captures: Number of distinct atomic sub-operations in the task. Derived cache field; never hand-set.
schema.allowed_values: Integer >=1. Derived = len(sub_operations).
schema.why: Skill-richness proxy orthogonal to horizon (a task can be long but skill-poor, or short but skill-dense).

schema.captures: Exact set of connector tools invoked in the golden trajectory, from the fixed 15-tool roster.
schema.allowed_values: Subset of: photo_grade_restore, color_white_balance, masking, background_removal, retouch, crop_resize, preset_looks, duotone_stylize, vectorize, screenprint_separations, stock_licensing, data_merge_layout, template_fill, video_cut, audio_clean. Non-empty (>=1). BANNED capabilities (text_to_image, from_scratch_art, upscale, inpainting, generative_expand, font_design) MUST NOT appear — presence fails metadata-correctness.
schema.why: Ground-truth tool-coverage map for portfolio balance, feasibility auditing (banned tool = reject), and buyer filtering by required tool.

schema.captures: Number of distinct connector tools invoked. Derived cache field; never hand-set.
schema.allowed_values: Integer 1-15. Derived = len(tools_used).
schema.why: Coverage scalar guaranteed consistent with tools_used; feeds coverage dashboards.

schema.captures: Set of connector tool-categories spanned by tools_used, grouping the 15 tools into functional classes. Derived cache field.
schema.allowed_values: Subset of {tonal(photo_grade_restore,color_white_balance), selection(masking,background_removal), retouch(retouch,preset_looks), stylize(duotone_stylize), vector(vectorize,screenprint_separations), sourcing(stock_licensing), layout(data_merge_layout,template_fill), geometry(crop_resize), motion(video_cut,audio_clean)}, size 1-9. Derived from tools_used via the fixed tool->category map.
schema.why: Captures BREADTH of tooling type (4 tools in 1 category is easier than 4 across 4); the sole objective basis for tool_diversity.

schema.captures: Single dominant media class of the primary deliverable. Derived cache field for top-level filtering; never human-set.
schema.allowed_values: Exactly one of: raster_image | vector_art | print_document | screen_document | motion_video | audio | tabular_data. (print_document=paginated CMYK-intent PDF/INDD; screen_document=RGB deck/PDF/PPTX.) Derived from output_format[] + canvas_class via the fixed map.
schema.why: Cached top-level output map and family cross-check; every distinction (raster/vector/print-vs-screen doc) is recoverable from output_format+canvas_class, so it is a convenience field only.

schema.captures: Full set of distinct file formats the deliverable is delivered in.
schema.allowed_values: One or more of: JPEG | PNG | TIFF | PSD | PDF | PDF_X | SVG | EPS | AI | INDD | IDML | GIF | WEBP | MP4 | MOV | WAV | MP3 | PPTX | CSV. Closed set of 19; non-empty (every task ships >=1 file).
schema.why: Primary buyer filter ('all SVG/AI vector', 'only PDF/X print') and the source of the derived output_modality; lets us verify the format is connector-reachable.

schema.captures: Total number of distinct deliverable FILES the finished task must yield (all pages/rows/variations/formats counted; count files, not layers).
schema.allowed_values: Integer 1..5000 (1 = single asset; 500-row merge = 500; 3-format hero = 3). Distinct from page_count (structural pages within one document) and roster_record_count (data rows).
schema.why: Throughput/scale slice ('high-volume merge count>=50' vs 'single-hero'); prices volume work.

schema.captures: Number of distinct pages/artboards/spreads WITHIN a single multi-page document deliverable (structural composition depth, not file count).
schema.allowed_values: Integer 1..500 (1 = single-page/single-artboard; a 12-page catalog = 12; a 2-artboard business card front/back = 2). Counts pages of the primary document, NOT data-row exports (roster_record_count) and NOT format variants (output_count).
schema.why: Separates a 12-page catalog (deep composition/coordination load) from a 12-format export (mechanical multiplication) that both read output_count=12; drives multi-page-layout curriculum and print-production slicing.

schema.captures: What dimension the multiple outputs vary along, when output_count>1 (the axis contributing the most distinct outputs).
schema.allowed_values: none (count=1) | data_rows (per-record merge) | platform_sizes (same content, different canvas sizes) | format_exports (same design, different formats) | color_variants (palette options) | crop_orientations (portrait/landscape/square of one shot) | design_alternatives (distinct creative options). MULTI-AXIS TIE-BREAK (highest priority wins; divergence outranks mechanical multiplication): design_alternatives > color_variants > crop_orientations > platform_sizes > format_exports > data_rows > none.
schema.why: Separates '500 near-identical merge PDFs' from '3 divergent creative concepts' — critical for repetition-vs-divergence curriculum; the fixed priority order removes the coin-flip on mixed cases.

schema.captures: Intended consumption surface/medium of the primary deliverable's canvas (determined by deliverable_type via the fixed artifact->surface map; human overrides only when the brief explicitly repurposes the artifact).
schema.allowed_values: Exactly one of: print | web | social | video_frame | packaging | presentation | document_page.
schema.why: Core 'print vs web vs social' organizing axis; drives color_space/print_requirements and feeds derived output_modality. deliverable_surface dropped as a pure synonym — a coarser buyer bucket is a documented grouping over this tag, not a stored field.
schema.improves_on: deliverable_type (splits surface out of the free noun; absorbs the dropped deliverable_surface)

schema.captures: Aspect/orientation of the primary output canvas, from its dimensions.
schema.allowed_values: portrait (H>W) | landscape (W>H) | square (W==H within 5%) | panoramic (aspect >=2.39:1) | multi (spans several differing orientations; use only when no single orientation dominates).
schema.why: Enables crop/resize-heavy slices and social-set balancing; derived from output canvas dimensions so it is objective.

schema.captures: Required color model/profile of the primary deliverable, by a most-constraining-wins ladder.
schema.allowed_values: PRECEDENCE (most-constraining wins): spot_color (any named Pantone/spot ink present, incl. screen-print seps) > bitmap_1bit (pure 1-bit halftone output) > CMYK (print-intent, no spot) > grayscale (single-channel neutral) > display_p3 (brief explicitly names P3/wide-gamut) > sRGB (default). sRGB is the default whenever the brief names no profile and canvas_class is web/social/presentation.
schema.why: Print-readiness filter and brand-strictness driver; the full precedence ladder resolves p3-vs-sRGB and single-ink ambiguity flagged in audit.

schema.captures: Which physical-print production constraints the deliverable must satisfy (empty for screen-only).
schema.allowed_values: Zero or more of: bleed | crop_marks | safe_margin | min_dpi_300 | embedded_fonts | overprint_or_knockout | dieline. Empty = no print constraints (screen deliverable).
schema.why: The auditable 'print-specific booleans' buyers ask for; builds a print-production curriculum and per-constraint Layer-3 verifiers. Accessibility constraints live in accessibility_requirements, not here.

schema.captures: Output size/fidelity bracket of the primary raster or frame, by longest pixel edge (or DPI at final trim for print).
schema.allowed_values: T1_thumb (<=640px / low-res proof) | T2_web (641-1920px / 72-150 DPI) | T3_hd (1921-2560px, incl. 1080p-1440p frames) | T4_4k_print (2561-4096px or >=300 DPI print at final size, incl. 2160p) | T5_large_format (>4096px / banner / billboard) | n_a (pure vector/audio/data).
schema.why: Difficulty+feasibility signal (connector exports but cannot upscale, so T4/T5 must be inherently present vs max_input_resolution_class) and a high-res/large-format buyer slice.

schema.captures: Primary intended viewer/consumer segment the deliverable speaks to (the WHO, distinct from domain and formality_register).
schema.allowed_values: One of 10: mass_consumer | luxury_affluent | b2b_professional | youth_genz | family_parents | children | seniors | enthusiast_hobbyist | institutional_govt | creative_industry.
schema.why: Drives tone/visual expectations and audience-specific buyer slices; a bank can target youth or affluent, so it is orthogonal to domain.

schema.captures: Stable identifier of the fictional brand persona used by the task.
schema.allowed_values: UPPERCASE_SLUG of the fictional brand (e.g. DERMAVERA), or 'GENERIC' when the brief names no brand.
schema.why: Groups/dedupes tasks sharing a brand and keeps persona joinable to a separate brand-attributes table; normalizes the old free text.

schema.captures: Whether the brief supplies >=1 concrete brand asset/spec (logo files, named hex/Pantone, specified fonts, or an authored template) the output must conform to.
schema.allowed_values: true = >=1 concrete brand asset/spec present | false = no brand assets; cues are prose-only or absent.
schema.why: Cleanly separates 'conform to a supplied identity' from 'interpret freely'; the objective precondition for brand_strictness>=3 and gates the [T] template mode.

schema.captures: Which specific brand-identity elements the brief locks the agent to.
schema.allowed_values: Subset (0+) of: logo | palette | typography | spacing_grid | tone_voice | imagery_style | iconography | layout_template. Empty allowed.
schema.why: Turns brand_strictness into an itemized, verifiable checklist ('tasks where palette AND typography are locked') and grounds per-element verifiers.

schema.captures: How tightly the output is bound to a predefined brand identity, with per-level anchors and an objective level-5 trigger set.
schema.allowed_values: 1 = no brand constraints, agent free on all colors/fonts/layout; 2 = soft guidance only (a mood/adjective like 'warm, premium'), no fixed assets; 3 = some fixed elements supplied (logo OR palette) that must be preserved, rest free; 4 = full brand kit (logo + palette + fonts) all honored, minor layout latitude; 5 = strict compliance, assigned iff the brief specifies >=1 of {exact hex or Pantone code that must match, a named locked font used verbatim, mandated logo clear-space/minimum-size/grid, or explicit 'no deviation / must match exactly' language}. INVARIANT: brand_strictness=5 should co-occur with dealbreaker_present=true.
schema.why: THE headline curriculum + selling axis; brand_kit_provided sets the floor, the concrete trigger list removes the 4-vs-5 subjectivity flagged in audit.

schema.captures: Intended tone/voice register of the deliverable, parsed from the brief's tone adjectives and deliverable_type convention; independent of audience.
schema.allowed_values: 1 = playful/irreverent (memes, slang, youth energy); 2 = casual/friendly (conversational consumer); 3 = neutral/professional (clean default commercial); 4 = polished/premium (refined, aspirational luxury); 5 = formal/ceremonial (corporate, legal, institutional; certificates, invitations, annual reports). TIE-BREAK when cues conflict: the deliverable_type's conventional register wins (certificates/invitations/annual-reports default 5; memes/slang cues default 1) and human records the deciding cue.
schema.why: Drives font/color/whitespace choices; distinct from audience (a youth-luxury brand can be register 4); the parse target + tie-break remove ambiguity.

schema.captures: Time-of-year / datable season or holiday the deliverable is themed for (a datable season/holiday outranks a generic occasion).
schema.allowed_values: One of: none_evergreen | spring | summer | autumn | winter | holiday_yearend | valentines | halloween | back_to_school | new_year_fresh_start | ramadan_eid | lunar_new_year | pride | black_friday_sale | other_seasonal.
schema.why: Datable seasonal briefs impose palette/imagery/tone expectations to check and slice; splitting calendar from occasion removes the 'holiday product launch' double-value trap and the escape value prevents mis-tags.

schema.captures: Non-calendar campaign occasion the deliverable serves, independent of time-of-year.
schema.allowed_values: One of: none | product_launch | anniversary_milestone | sale_promotion | rebrand_launch | event_announcement | fundraiser | graduation | wedding_occasion | sporting_event | other_occasion.
schema.why: Lets a 'holiday product launch' carry BOTH its season (seasonal_calendar) and its occasion here without conflict; the escape value covers occasions not listed.

schema.captures: Primary natural language of the deliverable's copy, as ISO 639-1.
schema.allowed_values: en | es | fr | de | it | pt | nl | ja | zh | ko | ar | hi | multi (bilingual/multilingual). Default en.
schema.why: Text-heavy O5/template/screen-print tasks depend on script/direction (ar is RTL); balances language coverage. Separate from region_locale.

schema.captures: Target market region governing locale conventions (paper size, currency/number/date format, RTL, cultural norms).
schema.allowed_values: north_america | latin_america | uk_ireland | western_europe | nordics | eastern_europe | middle_east | africa | south_asia | east_asia | southeast_asia | oceania | global.
schema.why: Determines print specs (US Letter vs A4), data-merge formatting, RTL layout — all verifiable; balances a non-US-centric portfolio. Orthogonal to language.

schema.captures: Whether and how much textual copy the agent must place/set in the deliverable.
schema.allowed_values: none (imagery-only, no copy set) | minimal (1-3 short strings: a headline, a name, a price) | moderate (a headline + body block + several fields, e.g. a card or single record) | heavy (multi-block/multi-field copy dominating the layout, e.g. menu, catalog page, certificate body).
schema.why: Enables the 'text-heavy layout tasks' buyer slice and a copy-density curriculum; sub_operations:text_fill was too coarse to separate imagery jobs from typesetting-heavy jobs.

schema.captures: Origin and editorial constraint on the copy the agent sets (only meaningful when copy_presence != none).
schema.allowed_values: n_a (copy_presence=none) | supplied_verbatim (client copy that must be placed exactly, no edits) | supplied_editable (client copy the agent may lightly adjust/format) | agent_authored (agent writes the copy within brief guidance) | data_driven (copy comes from roster fields via data-merge).
schema.why: Verbatim vs authored is a first-order precision/honesty driver (mis-set supplied copy = dealbreaker); lets buyers isolate copywriting vs typesetting tasks.

schema.captures: Whether the copy includes legal/regulated text that must be reproduced exactly (certificates' legal wording, pharma claims, ingredient/allergen lists, financial disclaimers).
schema.allowed_values: true = >=1 legal/regulated/compliance string the deliverable must carry verbatim | false = no regulated text.
schema.why: Forces zero-tolerance precision and honesty checks (a mangled disclaimer is a hard fail); a high-value, sellable compliance slice invisible to sub_operations.

schema.captures: Upfront decomposition/ordering the agent must do, anchored to DEPENDENCY/ORDERING structure (a 'step' = one connector tool call, same unit as est_calls); count bands are illustrative, structure is decisive.
schema.allowed_values: 1 = single obvious action, no ordering (~1 step); 2 = fixed linear order, no real choice (~2-3 steps); 3 = >=1 hard dependency where wrong order fails (~4-6 steps); 4 = multiple dependencies + intermediate artifacts to track (~7-12 steps); 5 = parallel sub-goals needing reconciliation of intermediate outputs (~13+ steps). When count and dependency structure disagree, dependency structure sets the level.
schema.why: Core multi-step-reasoning curriculum axis; anchoring on dependency structure (not raw count) keeps it orthogonal to horizon_tier instead of re-encoding it.

schema.captures: Breadth of distinct tool categories the agent must correctly select among. Strictly derived; excluded from difficulty_tier double-counting only in name (it IS one of the 8 axes but computed from tool_category_mix).
schema.allowed_values: 1 = 1 tool category; 2 = 2 categories; 3 = 3-4 categories; 4 = 5-6 categories; 5 = 7+ categories. Derived by bucketing len(tool_category_mix).
schema.why: Tool-selection difficulty; derived from tool_category_mix so it is objective and cannot drift from the actual tools.

schema.captures: Number of distinct independently-placed visual elements to compose/keep aligned in the final artifact (per-record FIELDS counted once for template design, NOT once per row; background/canvas not counted).
schema.allowed_values: 1 = single element on plain ground; 2 = 2-4 elements, simple layout; 3 = 5-9 elements with alignment (e.g. headline+body+image+logo); 4 = 10-20 elements, multi-zone grid or multi-field record; 5 = 21+ elements or dense multi-page/multi-record layout. A countable element = any independently-placed object (each text block, image/logo, distinct shape/rule/icon).
schema.why: Composition-load slice distinct from planning; the element definition (fields-once, background excluded) removes overlap with roster_record_count and makes density objective.

schema.captures: How much open aesthetic decision-making the brief LEAVES to the agent (inverse of prescription); distinct from brief_ambiguity (which is about missing/contradictory spec, not permitted latitude).
schema.allowed_values: 1 = fully prescribed, every parameter given (execute exactly, one correct output); 2 = values given, minor spacing/crop leeway; 3 = direction given, agent picks specifics within brand rules; 4 = goal + mood given, agent chooses layout/palette accents/treatment; 5 = only objective/audience given, agent defines concept and full aesthetic.
schema.why: Separates deterministic-verifiable tasks from open design; feeds rl_suitability and auto_verifier_share expectations.

schema.captures: How UNDERSPECIFIED or internally contradictory the brief is (missing required values, conflicting instructions), independent of allowed creative latitude.
schema.allowed_values: 1 = complete & unambiguous, every needed spec present; 2 = one minor gap the agent can safely default (unstated margin); 3 = a material gap requiring a stated assumption (missing a size or one color); 4 = multiple gaps OR one internal contradiction to reconcile/flag; 5 = severely underspecified or self-contradictory, correct handling requires clarification/assumption-logging.
schema.why: Drives clarification/assumption behavior — a distinct instruction-following and honesty training slice; a brief can be highly prescriptive (creative_freedom=1) yet ambiguous, so it is orthogonal to creative_freedom.

schema.captures: The TIGHTEST tolerance the acceptance criteria impose on ANY single check (the strictest verifier sets the level; take the max strictness across all checks).
schema.allowed_values: 1 = all checks subjective (any reasonable look passes); 2 = tightest check ~+/-10% (eyeballed alignment); 3 = tightest check ~+/-5% / snap-to-grid / named color close enough; 4 = >=1 exact-value check (exact hex, exact dimension, pixel-accurate mask edge); 5 = >=1 zero-tolerance production check (bleed/trim to spec, color-managed exact hex, sub-pixel registration, verbatim legal text).
schema.why: Defines how strictly reward functions must score and enables high-precision RL slices; the max-strictness rule removes the mixed-tolerance ambiguity flagged in audit.

schema.captures: Whether the workflow is a fixed linear pipeline or requires conditional decisions on intermediate results.
schema.allowed_values: 1 = strictly linear, fixed steps; 2 = linear with one optional cleanup step; 3 = one conditional fork (e.g. remove-bg only if background is busy); 4 = multiple conditionals or per-item branching across a batch; 5 = adaptive loop: inspect, decide, re-plan repeatedly.
schema.why: Separates deterministic pipelines from agentic decision-making for RL environment design.

schema.captures: How costly/irreversible a wrong action is within the task.
schema.allowed_values: 1 = fully reversible, non-destructive only; 2 = reversible but re-running is slow/costly (async render); 3 = one destructive step with easy redo (flatten, crop with original kept); 4 = destructive step hard to redo (masked recolor baked in, separations output); 5 = irreversible high-stakes (final print-ready to spec, licensed-stock spend, batch overwrite of many files).
schema.why: Flags where a single mistake is expensive — key for RL safety-shaping and weighting expert-review effort.

schema.captures: Number of connector tool invocations to complete the task (one image_adjust call = 1; a 3-format export = 3 export calls).
schema.allowed_values: Integer 1-60. When has_golden_trajectory=true this EQUALS the length of the golden trajectory's tool-call list (auto); when false, estimate at that same tool-call granularity (auto+human).
schema.why: Raw horizon measure feeding horizon_tier and effort pricing; pinning the count to connector-call granularity removes the 2-3x tagger drift and makes it auto where a trajectory exists.

schema.captures: Banded task length derived from est_calls. Derived cache field.
schema.why: Length band for buyer slices and long-horizon RL episode-budget selection; derived from est_calls so it stays consistent.

schema.captures: Overall rolled-up difficulty band: arithmetic mean of the 8 ordinal axes (each integer 1-5), computed to two decimals, band edges inclusive-lower/exclusive-upper. Any axis tagged n_a is excluded and the divisor adjusted.
schema.allowed_values: T1_intro (mean <2.00) | T2_basic (2.00-2.79) | T3_intermediate (2.80-3.59) | T4_advanced (3.60-4.20) | T5_expert (>4.20). Bands are contiguous over the 1-5 range (two-decimal mean). The 8 axes = planning_complexity, tool_diversity, visual_density, creative_freedom, precision_tolerance, branching, reversibility_risk, brand_strictness.
schema.why: Single sortable knob for the difficulty curriculum and 'expert-tier only' buyers; the precise mean/rounding rule makes it reproducible once the underlying axes are tightened per the fixes above.

schema.captures: Whether the task is fully doable with permitted connectors, given the banned-capability list (the single doable/not-doable verdict).
schema.allowed_values: full = end-to-end with permitted tools, all needed assets supplied or sourceable | template = doable only via an authored .indd/.ai template that must be present | partial = core doable but a required intermediate must be derived by a permitted tool (e.g. vectorize a logo, produce separations, license a hero) or some sub-steps are out of scope | no = requires a BANNED capability (text_to_image, from_scratch_art, upscale, inpainting, generative_expand, font_design). Transient by-products (masks, cutouts) never lower feasibility below full.
schema.why: Admission gatekeeper and RL reward-shaping filter; anchors honesty_relevant. Gives one clean doable/not-doable verdict per task.

schema.captures: The connector runtime path(s) the task requires to run end-to-end.
schema.allowed_values: Subset of {C_headless (fully programmatic), W_interactive_express (needs interactive Express authoring), A_async_av (async video/audio render/enhance), T_authored_template (fill a pre-authored template)}. Non-empty.
schema.why: Routes tasks to the right runner and flags which need human-in-loop Express; critical for has_golden_trajectory and rl_suitability.

schema.captures: Which of the 7 scoring capability buckets the task exercises; a bucket is IN the footprint iff >=1 authored verifier is tagged to it (membership derived from verifier bucket labels, not a human read).
schema.allowed_values: Subset of {Instruction Following, Asset Utilization, Brand Alignment, Technical Quality, Creative Quality, Process, Honesty}.
schema.why: Master scoring-coverage map buyers use to guarantee a capability is represented; deriving membership from verifier labels gives |capability_footprint| a stable meaning for downstream tiers (value set kept fixed per instruction).
schema.example: [Instruction Following, Asset Utilization, Brand Alignment, Technical Quality, Process, Honesty]

schema.captures: The single capability bucket carrying the most scoring weight = the bucket to which the largest share of the task's Layer-3 verifier points is assigned (ties broken by dealbreaker location).
schema.allowed_values: Instruction Following | Asset Utilization | Brand Alignment | Technical Quality | Creative Quality | Process | Honesty. CONSTRAINT: must be a member of capability_footprint.
schema.why: Lets buyers buy tasks that primarily TEST a capability (not just touch it) and lets curriculum weight training; defining weight by verifier points makes it derived not human, and the membership constraint prevents contradiction with capability_footprint.

schema.captures: Number of authored Layer-3 verifiers attached to the task.
schema.allowed_values: Integer 0-60.
schema.why: Density of ground-truth checks; a sellability/quality signal and the denominator for auto_verifier_share.

schema.captures: Fraction of this task's verifiers that are machine-checkable without a human rater. Derived cache field.
schema.allowed_values: Float 0.0-1.0 = auto-checkable verifiers / verifier_count (0.0 when verifier_count=0).
schema.why: Predicts RL-reward reliability and eval automatability; high share = cheap-to-run environment; feeds rl_suitability.

schema.captures: Whether the task has >=1 dealbreaker verifier that fails the whole task if violated.
schema.allowed_values: true | false. INVARIANT: brand_strictness=5 => dealbreaker_present=true.
schema.why: Marks hard pass/fail gates (e.g. off-brand color, mangled legal text) for strict-eval slices; derived from the verifier set so it stays consistent with brand_strictness.

schema.captures: Whether correct handling requires refusing, flagging infeasibility, or disclosing a limitation. Derived cache field.
schema.allowed_values: true | false. Derived: true iff feasibility in {partial, no} OR a banned sub-request is embedded OR brief_ambiguity>=4.
schema.why: Isolates the honesty/refusal training and eval slice; derived from feasibility + brief_ambiguity so it is objective.

schema.captures: Which auditable accessibility constraints the deliverable must satisfy (empty when none apply).
schema.allowed_values: Zero or more of: min_contrast_ratio | min_legible_type_size | colorblind_safe_palette | alt_text_provided | plain_language | rtl_correctness. Empty = no accessibility constraints stated.
schema.why: Real institutional/medical/finance deliverables carry these verifiable constraints; a sellable accessibility slice invisible in the draft (print_requirements covers only physical production).

schema.captures: Licensing status of the assets the task depends on, by concrete provenance triggers.
schema.allowed_values: clean = every asset has a recorded synthetic/owned provenance | stock_required = the only external dependency is stock that WILL be licensed through the connector (license capturable) | risk = any supplied asset lacks a documented synthetic/owned origin OR carries an unverified third-party source OR is a stock asset without a captured license record.
schema.why: Drives redistributability and legal review; the concrete trigger tests remove the 'uncertain provenance' guesswork so redistributable is a stable bright line.

schema.captures: Whether the task and its assets can be resold/redistributed as training data without restriction. Derived cache field.
schema.allowed_values: true | false. Derived: true iff license_class = clean.
schema.why: Bright-line sellability gate; derived from license_class so it never contradicts the rights status; feeds sft_value_tier.

schema.captures: Strength of the task as an RL environment, by an explicit derivation table over auto_verifier_share, execution_mode, and creative_freedom.
schema.allowed_values: 5 iff auto_verifier_share>=0.9 AND execution_mode=={C_headless} AND creative_freedom<=2; else 4 iff auto_verifier_share>=0.7 AND C_headless in execution_mode AND creative_freedom<=3; else 3 iff auto_verifier_share>=0.4 AND task is headless-runnable (C_headless in execution_mode); else 2 iff auto_verifier_share>0; else 1 (auto_verifier_share==0 OR not headless-runnable with creative_freedom>=4). First matching rule wins.
schema.why: Selects the RL-environment slice; the exact threshold table makes it truly derived, removing the 'derived in name only' flag.

schema.captures: Overall value of the task as an SFT training target, by numeric-bound rules (first matching rule wins, top-down).
schema.allowed_values: A = has_golden_trajectory AND redistributable AND operation_count>=2 AND |capability_footprint|>=5; else B = has_golden_trajectory AND redistributable AND |capability_footprint| in 3-4; else C = has_golden_trajectory AND (operation_count==1 OR |capability_footprint|<=2); else D = NOT has_golden_trajectory OR NOT redistributable.
schema.why: Headline SFT sellability tier; binding the fuzzy coverage words to counts makes the derived tier reproducible.

schema.captures: Whether a verified end-to-end golden agent trajectory exists for the task.
schema.allowed_values: true | false.
schema.why: Gates SFT-target usability and feeds est_calls (auto when true) and sft_value_tier; buyers filter for tasks that ship a demonstrated solution path.

schema.captures: Identifier of the author/source that created the task, for traceability.
schema.allowed_values: Free slug of the author or generation source (e.g. AUTHOR_ANKIT, BATCH_FLAGSHIP_R3, MEGA_BENCH_V21), or 'UNKNOWN' when untracked.
schema.why: Provenance/audit trail for a sold data product; lets curation trace clusters back to an author or generation batch and dedupe by source.

schema.captures: Identifier of the near-duplicate cluster this task belongs to (same operation+domain+deliverable recombination); unique id = singleton/novel.
schema.allowed_values: Cluster slug/hash shared by near-duplicate briefs (e.g. CLUST_O5_COFFEE_MENU), or a unique per-task id when the task has no near-duplicate.
schema.why: When scaling to 100+, lets curation guarantee diversity and lets buyers avoid paying for redundant clones that domain/operation slices alone cannot surface.

schema.captures: Estimated expert annotation/QA effort to score one completed attempt, as a T-shirt bucket (orthogonal to est_calls — a short task can be expensive to judge).
schema.allowed_values: XS (<15 min, mostly auto-checked) | S (15-30 min) | M (30-60 min) | L (1-2 hr, subjective/creative judgment) | XL (>2 hr, deep multi-facet expert review).
schema.why: Core economics for pricing tasks and balancing a portfolio by cost; verifier_count is a proxy but not effort, and this drives the annotation budget.

current_mapping.becomes: domain (closed 22-value enum with 'other') + new sub_domain for the niche level, now with the FULL 22-domain->sub_domain table published inline (each list ending in an 'other_<domain>' escape)

deliverable_type (closed 24-value artifact enum with an 'other'+note rule and disambiguation notes) + canvas_class (derived surface via a fixed artifact->surface map); deliverable_surface DROPPED as a pure synonym; free-noun surface/artifact mixing removed

primary_operation (computable tie-break) + secondary_operations[] (bright-line, crop/resize excluded) + operation_count + sub_operations[] (closed vocab, now also carries the needs_derivation signal) + sub_operation_count
operation_family_set (multi-enum spanned families, derived from primary+secondary ops; single-value family retired)
output_modality (7-class enum, now strictly DERIVED from output_format[]+canvas_class as a cached convenience field) + output_format[] (19 concrete formats, the auto source)
horizon_tier (H1-H4 with explicit call-count cutoffs, derived from est_calls)
est_calls (counting rule pinned to connector-call granularity; equals golden-trajectory length and becomes auto when has_golden_trajectory=true)
feasibility (full/template/partial/no, 'no' tied to the banned list, 'partial' now absorbs the needs_derivation gap; input_completeness DROPPED to remove the overlap)
tools_used[] (named 15-tool roster, banned tools forbidden) + tool_count (derived) + tool_category_mix[] (derived breadth); count no longer floats free of the tool list
brand_persona_id (slug) + domain_audience (WHO) + formality_register (HOW-formal, with parse target + tie-break) + brand_kit_provided + brand_elements_constrained[]; free text fully decomposed

capability_footprint (7 buckets, value set unchanged, but membership now DERIVED from verifier bucket labels) + new scoring_bucket_primary (derived from verifier-point share, constrained to be a member of the footprint)
dropped; cross-modality is readable from operation_family_set (size>1) and output_format[]/output_modality, making a separate boolean redundant
planning_complexity (1-5 anchored on DEPENDENCY/ORDERING structure with 'step'=connector call, count bands illustrative only, so it no longer re-encodes horizon)
brand_strictness (1-5; floor set by brand_kit_provided, itemized by brand_elements_constrained, level 5 given an objective trigger set + dealbreaker co-occurrence invariant)
tool_diversity (1-5 strictly derived by bucketing len(tool_category_mix))
visual_density (1-5 with a defined countable element — fields-once, background excluded — removing overlap with roster_record_count; size split off into resolution_tier)
creative_freedom (1-5 prescription anchors) + new brief_ambiguity split out to capture under-specification separately
